findings.geneticAnalysis: A derived CT call is available for MTHFR C677T. This is CDC-framed folate-processing context only and is not a toxin-sensitivity result, detoxification-capacity result, methylation score, diagnosis, diet recommendation, supplement recommendation, medication result, treatment result, or actionability claim.
findings.sourceLabel: CDC MTHFR Gene Variant and Folic Acid Facts
findings.plainEnglishMeaning: CDC framing is used here: common MTHFR variants can be discussed as folate-processing context, but they do not by themselves support toxin-sensitivity, detox, diet, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, or actionability conclusions.

findings.geneticAnalysis: A derived AC call is available for MTHFR A1298C. This marker is reported as local context only and does not infer toxin sensitivity, detoxification capacity, methylation capacity, homocysteine, lab status, diet needs, supplement needs, medication response, diagnosis, treatment needs, or actionability.

findings.plainEnglishMeaning: This row keeps the local genotype visible while leaving toxin sensitivity, detoxification, lab, diet, supplement, medication, diagnosis, treatment, and actionability interpretations unavailable without validated evidence.

folateContext.plainEnglishMeaning: The available MTHFR calls can be described as folate-processing context, not as a toxin-sensitivity measurement.
folateContext.folicAcidSafetyNote: Under the CDC framing used by this package, common MTHFR variants are not a reason to avoid folic acid.
